findings.description: Overall page layout is significantly altered from a cover page to a top header.
findings.evidence: The PDF features centered text with large vertical gaps positioned centrally on the page. The HTML left-aligns the text at the top of the page and adds an extraneous horizontal line, changing the visual structure.

findings.description: Loss of original text formatting, including font size, alignment, and line breaks.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses large, centered fonts with intentional line breaks within the text blocks (e.g., 'Hertford County CAMA Land' / 'Use Plan Update'). The HTML uses much smaller, left-aligned fonts and concatenates the text blocks into single lines.
